plan.title: refresh-01: make workspace refresh behavior sound and optimized by explaining and reducing full refreshes, fixing pathological deferred behavior, and tightening overall refresh performance semantics
plan.goal: refresh-01: make workspace refresh behavior sound and optimized by explaining and reducing full refreshes, fixing pathological deferred behavior, and tightening overall refresh performance semantics

plan.title: refresh-01: make workspace refresh behavior sound and optimized by explaining and reducing full refreshes, fixing pathological deferred behavior, and tightening overall refresh performance semantics
plan.goal: refresh-01: make workspace refresh behavior sound and optimized by explaining and reducing full refreshes, fixing pathological deferred behavior, and tightening overall refresh performance semantics

node.title: Baseline the live refresh paths and quantify full vs deferred triggers
node.summary: Capture the current refresh mix, slow-path timings, and concrete trigger conditions for full rescans and deferred runtime sync under active repo churn.

acceptance.label: Refresh samples identify where full rescans are coming from and which subphase dominates the slow path.
Deferred behavior is broken down into fs refresh, snapshot, and reload timing with a clear pathology statement.

plan.title: refresh-01: make workspace refresh behavior sound and optimized by explaining and reducing full refreshes, fixing pathological deferred behavior, and tightening overall refresh performance semantics
plan.goal: refresh-01: make workspace refresh behavior sound and optimized by explaining and reducing full refreshes, fixing pathological deferred behavior, and tightening overall refresh performance semantics

node.title: Eliminate unnecessary full refreshes and reduce unavoidable rescan cost
node.summary: Tighten dirty-path and fallback behavior so full rescans are rare, and optimize the remaining full-scan path so the rare cases are materially faster.

acceptance.label: Unnecessary full refresh triggers are removed or downgraded to scoped incremental refreshes.
Remaining full refreshes have a justified trigger model and materially lower latency.

plan.title: refresh-01: make workspace refresh behavior sound and optimized by explaining and reducing full refreshes, fixing pathological deferred behavior, and tightening overall refresh performance semantics
plan.goal: refresh-01: make workspace refresh behavior sound and optimized by explaining and reducing full refreshes, fixing pathological deferred behavior, and tightening overall refresh performance semantics

node.title: Fix pathological deferred refresh behavior
node.summary: Explain why deferred refresh/runtime-sync calls are repeating or stalling, then remove the pathological cases and make the remaining deferred path predictable and cheap.

acceptance.label: Deferred refreshes no longer spin or stall pathologically under active edits.
Slow deferred calls have a clear bounded cause with corrected behavior or explicit fallbacks.

plan.title: refresh-01: make workspace refresh behavior sound and optimized by explaining and reducing full refreshes, fixing pathological deferred behavior, and tightening overall refresh performance semantics
plan.goal: refresh-01: make workspace refresh behavior sound and optimized by explaining and reducing full refreshes, fixing pathological deferred behavior, and tightening overall refresh performance semantics

node.title: Harden the refresh state machine and fallback semantics
node.summary: Review the end-to-end refresh model so watch-driven, fallback, runtime-sync, and background refresh behavior have sound boundaries and do not regress back into broad rescans or hidden stalls.

acceptance.label: Refresh path semantics are coherent and intentionally separated between scoped refresh, rescan, and deferred runtime sync.
Fallback behavior is minimal, justified, and measurable.

plan.title: refresh-01: make workspace refresh behavior sound and optimized by explaining and reducing full refreshes, fixing pathological deferred behavior, and tightening overall refresh performance semantics
plan.goal: refresh-01: make workspace refresh behavior sound and optimized by explaining and reducing full refreshes, fixing pathological deferred behavior, and tightening overall refresh performance semantics

node.title: Validate refresh performance and regression coverage
node.summary: Prove that refresh behavior is sound after the fixes with targeted runtime validation, representative edit scenarios, and updated regression coverage.

acceptance.label: Validation demonstrates fewer or rarer full refreshes, corrected deferred behavior, and improved latency.
Regression coverage protects the intended refresh semantics going forward.

node.title: Baseline the live refresh paths and quantify full vs deferred triggers
node.summary: Baseline captured from live runtimeStatus/runtimeLogs and source inspection: a clean-state fallback probe in `refresh_fs_nonblocking` was surfacing `DeferredBusy` while `fsObserved == fsApplied`, producing false deferred refresh events; recent live full refreshes were about 2563 ms and repeated deferred refreshes also showed snapshot-revision holds up to about 20.9s and 22.7s.

acceptance.label: Refresh samples identify where full rescans are coming from and which subphase dominates the slow path.
Deferred behavior is broken down into fs refresh, snapshot, and reload timing with a clear pathology statement.

node.title: Fix pathological deferred refresh behavior
node.summary: Started removing one deferred-path pathology by making busy clean-state fallback probes stay clean and by keeping fallback scans behind the refresh lock. Remaining work should focus on repeated dirty-state deferred cycles and the long snapshot-revision holds visible in live runtime refresh logs.

acceptance.label: Deferred refreshes no longer spin or stall pathologically under active edits.
Slow deferred calls have a clear bounded cause with corrected behavior or explicit fallbacks.

node.title: Fix pathological deferred refresh behavior
node.summary: Deferred refresh/runtime-sync handling now has two concrete fixes: `prism-core` no longer reports busy clean-state fallback probes as deferred, and `prism-mcp` now short-circuits read/background/persisted deferred paths before revision reload work. Focus next on live dirty-workspace validation and any remaining long deferred observations that survive after the new release daemon restarts.

acceptance.label: Deferred refreshes no longer spin or stall pathologically under active edits.
Slow deferred calls have a clear bounded cause with corrected behavior or explicit fallbacks.

node.title: Fix pathological deferred refresh behavior
node.summary: Eliminated the remaining pathological deferred-refresh admission path by making coordination, claim, and artifact mutations fail fast on busy runtime-sync and refresh locks. Added regression coverage for traced coordination admission-busy behavior and claim admission-busy behavior, then rebuilt and restarted the release daemon. Live dirty-worktree validation now shows deferred status with 0 ms hold instead of the earlier multi-second deferred runtime-sync hold.

acceptance.label: Deferred refreshes no longer spin or stall pathologically under active edits.
Slow deferred calls have a clear bounded cause with corrected behavior or explicit fallbacks.

node.title: Eliminate unnecessary full refreshes and reduce unavoidable rescan cost
node.summary: Tighten dirty-path and fallback behavior so full rescans are rare, and optimize the remaining full-scan path so the rare cases are materially faster.

acceptance.label: Unnecessary full refresh triggers are removed or downgraded to scoped incremental refreshes.
Remaining full refreshes have a justified trigger model and materially lower latency.

node.title: Eliminate unnecessary full refreshes and reduce unavoidable rescan cost
node.summary: Reduced unavoidable fallback full-refresh cost by teaching `WorkspaceTreeSnapshot` to persist directory stat metadata and using cached file fingerprints plus selective changed-directory subtree scans during `plan_full_refresh`. Added focused core/store regressions, rebuilt the release daemon, and verified live full-refresh cost dropped to about 1215 ms from earlier samples at 2549 ms, 6217 ms, and 25974 ms.

acceptance.label: Unnecessary full refresh triggers are removed or downgraded to scoped incremental refreshes.
Remaining full refreshes have a justified trigger model and materially lower latency.

node.title: Harden the refresh state machine and fallback semantics
node.summary: Review the end-to-end refresh model so watch-driven, fallback, runtime-sync, and background refresh behavior have sound boundaries and do not regress back into broad rescans or hidden stalls.

acceptance.label: Refresh path semantics are coherent and intentionally separated between scoped refresh, rescan, and deferred runtime sync.
Fallback behavior is minimal, justified, and measurable.

node.title: Harden the refresh state machine and fallback semantics
node.summary: Separated fallback cached rescans from true full rebuilds by introducing a distinct `rescan` refresh mode/status through prism-core and prism-mcp. Fallback snapshot diff paths now record `rescan` in session/runtime status and keep `fullRebuildCount`/`workspaceReloaded` reserved for true full rebuilds, with focused regressions in prism-core and workspace_runtime plus a release rebuild/restart to refresh the live daemon.

acceptance.label: Refresh path semantics are coherent and intentionally separated between scoped refresh, rescan, and deferred runtime sync.
Fallback behavior is minimal, justified, and measurable.

node.title: Validate refresh performance and regression coverage
node.summary: Running a focused validation pass over the refreshed state-machine changes, including fallback `rescan` semantics in prism-core, runtime refresh telemetry in prism-mcp, and existing hot-path/deferred regressions that guard the broader plan outcomes.

acceptance.label: Validation demonstrates fewer or rarer full refreshes, corrected deferred behavior, and improved latency.
Regression coverage protects the intended refresh semantics going forward.

node.title: Validate refresh performance and regression coverage
node.summary: Validated the refresh plan end-to-end with focused regressions across the repaired surfaces: core watch-driven refresh still updates live session state, deferred runtime-sync reporting still skips reload work, the first mutation after a workspace refresh still avoids persisted reload, and persisted notes still reload when freshness requires it. Combined with the new `rescan` regressions and a healthy release rebuild/restart, the plan now has both semantic and behavioral coverage for the refreshed runtime path.

acceptance.label: Validation demonstrates fewer or rarer full refreshes, corrected deferred behavior, and improved latency.
Regression coverage protects the intended refresh semantics going forward.
